item.title: Why Google's Gemini Spark AI agent could be a game changer
item.description: AI tools that autonomously perform tasks for users mark a major step forward from chatbots, according to tech experts.

item.title: Trump postponing AI executive order "because I didn't like what I was seeing"
item.description: President Trump said he doesn't want to "do anything that's going to get in the way" of leading the world on the technology.

item.title: Epstein island trespasser says he was hog-tied, thrown into "dungeon"
item.description: Little St. James in the Virgin Islands has attracted seekers lured by tales of Jeffrey Epstein's private island.

item.title: GOP Sen. Thom Tillis calls DOJ "anti-weaponization" fund a "payout pot for punks"
item.description: Congress left town without voting on what the Justice Department has called its "anti-weaponization" compensation fund. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: U.S. imposes new travel restrictions over Ebola outbreak in Congo
item.description: The U.S. is now refusing entry to any passenger without an American passport who has been to the Congo, Uganda, or South Sudan in the past three weeks. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder, an infectious disease specialist who treated patients during the West African Ebola epidemic, joins to discuss the latest on the outbreak.

item.title: How Ring's Fire Watch feature works to keep people safe amid wildfires
item.description: As thousands evacuate their homes in Southern California to flee the Sandy Fire, Ring doorbell and security cameras are helping residents and emergency crews stay informed and stay safe. Ring founder Jamie Siminoff joins CBS News to discuss the Fire Watch feature.

item.title: Stanford student explains how AI impacted his graduating class
item.description: A recent opinion piece in The New York Times spotlighted the impact of artificial intelligence on the 2026 graduating class at one of the world's most prestigious universities. The author, Stanford student Theo Baker, joins to discuss.

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden-era environmental rule on cooling units
item.description: An executive order signed by President Trump on Thursday loosens a federal rule requiring grocery stores and air conditioning companies to reduce greenhouse gas emissions from cooling equipment. CBS News correspondent Natalie Brand has more.

item.title: Iran mulls latest U.S. proposal as Trump says war is in "final stages"
item.description: Iran says it's considering the latest U.S. peace offer, as President Trump says he's willing to wait "a couple of days" for a response.

item.title: Senate GOP delays vote to fund immigration agencies amid DOJ "anti-weaponization" fund opposition
item.description: Senate Republicans delayed a vote to fund federal immigration agencies on Thursday due to issues with the Justice Department's "anti-weaponization" fund. CBS News' Nikole Killion and Sarah Lynch have the latest.

item.title: Zelenskyy confirmed his most famous quote. I got pushback for reporting it.
item.description: Whether Zelenskyy had in fact uttered the line "I need ammunition — not a ride" was disputed by the Biden administration when I reported it. Now, the Ukrainian leader has confirmed he said it.

item.title: House bill would ban use of federal money for DOJ's "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: GOP Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick and Democratic Rep. Tom Suozzi introduced a bill to ban the use of federal money for paying out claims under the Justice Department's new "anti-weaponization" fund.

item.title: Marjorie Taylor Greene says foreign donors are a "threat to the American people"
item.description: Former congresswoman Marjorie Taylor Greene says Rep. Thomas Massie's primary loss in Kentucky this week is proof "that a congressional seat can be bought by several billionaires representing a foreign lobby." See the full interview Thursday on "The Takeout."

item.title: All 24 "Survivor 50" castaways reflect on eliminations, historic season
item.description: Throughout the airing of "Survivor 50," castaways joined "CBS Mornings" to discuss their time on the show. In this marathon, relive the twists and turns and hear from all 24 contestants about what it was like to be a part of the 50th season.

item.title: Tennessee stops execution after failing to find vein for lethal drugs
item.description: Tennessee called off the planned execution of Tony Carruthers on Thursday, his attorney said.

item.title: Consumers are still spending, but cracks are starting to show
item.description: Shoppers continue to open their pocketbooks, boosting retailers like Walmart, even as inflation jumps to its highest level in three years.

item.title: DNC releases long-awaited autopsy on 2024 election
item.description: The Democratic National Committee released a long-awaited autopsy on the 2024 election that party chair Ken Martin has kept under wraps for months.

item.title: Comer says Jeffrey Epstein's former assistant gave three new names of people involved in abuse
item.description: House Oversight Committee chairman James Comer told reporters Thursday that Jeffrey Epstein's former personal assistant, Sarah Kellen, gave three new names of "people that were involved in abuse."

item.title: 7th and 8th judges rebuff Justice Dept.'s attempts to get voter rolls from states
item.description: The Justice Department has sued 30 states and Washington, D.C., in an effort to gain access to their complete voter registration lists.

item.title: "Arc de Trump" design plan approved, without lions on the base
item.description: The commissioners, all appointed by Trump, acted despite overwhelming public opposition to the 250-foot arch.

item.title: Colbert hosts "The Late Show" one last time tonight as franchise ends
item.description: "The Late Show" host Stephen Colbert is marking the end of an iconic late-night franchise on CBS.

item.title: U.S.-bound flight diverted over passenger from Congo amid Ebola restrictions
item.description: An Air France flight from Paris to Detroit was forced to divert to Montreal due to U.S. flight restrictions linked to the Ebola outbreak.

item.title: Atlantic hurricane season is coming up. Here's what NOAA predicts.
item.description: The 2026 Atlantic hurricane season is quickly approaching, and the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration is releasing its forecast for what to expect.

item.title: "Survivor 50" finalists discuss strategy, how they planned to win
item.description: Aubry won the record $2 million prize for earning 8-of-11 votes in Wednesday night's finale of "Survivor 50."

item.title: U.S. doctor with Ebola feared he "wasn't going to make it" before evacuation
item.description: Dr. Peter Stafford was working with the missionary group Serge in Congo when he was infected with Ebola.

item.title: Arrest made in Connecticut 40 years after woman's murder in Virginia
item.description: The body of 22-year-old Roberta Walls was found in a field in Virginia Beach on the morning of May 15, 1986.

item.title: Harvard will cap number of A's awarded to students. Here's why.
item.description: Harvard University faculty members voted to cap the number of A's awarded to students in an effort to stop grade inflation.

item.title: "Peanuts" music owner sues U.S. government over use of iconic songs
item.description: Lee Mendelson Film Productions alleges the U.S. Department of the Interior illegally used the jazzy tunes in social media posts and a video game.

item.title: DOJ veterans fear probe into ex-CIA director is being stacked with Trump loyalists
item.description: Former CIA Director John Brennan is the subject of two criminal probes being led by the Miami-area U.S. Attorney's Office.

item.title: Poisoned cheesecake used as a weapon a first for NY investigators
item.description: Viktoria Nasyrova attempted to murder a woman with cheesecake. As one private investigator would find out, she had a list of alleged victims — including her ex-boyfriend's dog.
